task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas, treats any run of consecutive commas as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the criteria, although it does not explicitly name an edge case.
trace: The function first replaces consecutive commas with a single comma to ensure that run of consecutive commas are treated as one split. Then it splits the string on commas, drops any empty pieces at the start and end, and finally rejoins the remaining pieces with a single comma between each. An edge case handled is when there are multiple consecutive empty entries in the original string; after replacing them with a single comma, they will be properly ignored during splitting.
